brief_notes: Excellent benchmarking study with clear methodology and strong experimental design. The pseudo-real variant truthset approach using closely related genomes is well-motivated and avoids simulation artifacts. Comprehensive comparison across 7 ONT callers, 3 basecalling models, 2 read types, multiple depths, and 14 species provides high confidence in the conclusions. Key finding that 10x ONT depth matches Illumina is practically important. Good analysis of error sources (repetitive regions, homopolymers). Weaknesses: Limited to bacterial genomes so generalizability to eukaryotes is unclear; computational resource analysis is useful but brief; some discussion of structural variant calling would round out the paper. Writing is clear and well-organized throughout.
